text.title: Cloud Commitment Discounts: RIs, Savings Plans, and Spot Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real finops-devops-cost-commitments deployments, each paired with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: PostgreSQL or Redis runs on Spot instances; nightly outage during reclaim.
antipattern.root-cause: 2-minute termination notice is insufficient for graceful database shutdown.
antipattern.fix: Move stateful services to On-Demand or Reserved; Spot only for stateless / checkpointed workloads.

antipattern.symptom: Spot fleet collapses simultaneously when the provider reclaims the single pool.
antipattern.root-cause: Single instance type + single AZ in the Spot configuration.
antipattern.fix: Diversify across 3+ instance types and 2+ AZs to keep fleet interruption < 1%.

antipattern.symptom: RIs cover oversized instances; rightsizing later strands the discount.
antipattern.root-cause: Commitment purchased before the rightsizing pass.
antipattern.fix: Run rightsizing first; commit to the post-rightsize baseline only.

antipattern.symptom: Savings Plan utilisation drifts below 80% after a business slowdown.
antipattern.root-cause: Coverage set to the 90-day average instead of the 5th percentile floor.
antipattern.fix: Recalculate to 5th percentile; reduce coverage to 50-60% of the floor at renewal.
